answer:
The overall 5-year survival for Stage I patients was 63%, with T1N0 having 66% and T2N0 having 55%, indicating that while survival is relatively high, it is not uniformly excellent. The presence of significant prognostic factors like histology and tumor size further shows that not all Stage I cases have an equally favorable outcome. Therefore, Stage I non-small cell lung carcinoma may not always be considered truly "early" in terms of cure probability.

\boxed{C}
